draft.introduction: Jesus’ invitation—“Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest”—is one of the most personal and consoling passages in the Gospels.

translation.text: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
translation.original_text: δεῦτε πρός με πάντες οἱ κοπιῶντες καὶ πεφορτισμένοι

translator_notes.note: The “yoke” (ζυγός) was a standard rabbinic metaphor for Torah obedience. When Jesus says “take my yoke,” he substitutes his own teaching authority for the Torah’s—a claim of extraordinary audacity in a Jewish context.
